---
title: Module Planning Meeting
type: transcript
date: 2026-04-01
module: AI and Collective Intelligence
module_code: MASC-AICI-01
---

Meeting attendees: Dr. Sarah Chen (module lead), James Taylor (teaching assistant), Niccolò Pescetelli (programme director)

Sarah: Thanks for joining everyone. Let's start with the assessment timeline. I've drafted the brief for the group project. It's almost ready but I need feedback from both of you before we publish it.

Niccolò: When do we need it out by?

Sarah: Ideally by Friday the 10th of April. Students need at least three weeks before the deadline.

James: I can review it by Wednesday. Should I also update the rubric?

Sarah: Yes please. Make sure the rubric aligns with the learning outcomes we revised last week.

Niccolò: Good. What about the guest speaker for Week 5?

Sarah: I've confirmed Dr. Maria Lopez from UCL. She'll present on swarm intelligence and collective decision-making. I need someone to prepare a short bio and introduction for her.

James: I can do that. I'll draft it by next Monday.

Niccolò: Perfect. One more thing — a few students have asked about extensions for the individual essay. What's our policy?

Sarah: The standard policy applies — they need to apply through mitigating circumstances. We shouldn't be granting informal extensions. Let's make sure that's clear in the next communication.

Niccolò: Agreed. I'll draft a note for the student update this week. Anything else?

Sarah: Just one thing — I think we should extend the Week 6 workshop from two hours to three. The students needed more time last year. But I'm not sure about room availability.

Niccolò: Let's check with facilities. I'll email them today.

Sarah: Great. That's everything from me.

James: Same here. Thanks all.
